name: Vacuum Tube Electronics
tagline: Thermionic emission in evacuated envelopes — amplification and switching before solid-state junctions dominated
problem:
Crystals could detect radio waves, but stable amplification eluded early builders until thermionic valves appeared: heat a cathode until it boils electrons, pull them across vacuum with a positive plate, and modulate the flow with a grid that demands almost no power — gain from geometry and temperature, not from microscopic junctions yet.

Vacuum is the unsung hero and villain. Residual gas ionises under high fields; getters swallow molecules after seal-off; pumps must reach hard vacuum before the oxide cathode dies from poisoning. Tubes are hot, fragile, and microphonic — mechanical vibration becomes signal.

Still, tubes taught entire generations how amplifiers think: bias points, load lines, and screen grids are portable metaphors even after silicon arrives. High-power radio and radar kept tubes relevant where semiconductors struggled with voltage and heat.

For recovery, glassblowing, pump science, and getter flashing are crafts as essential as winding transformers — lose them and you lose long-distance voice until someone relearns them painfully.
overview:
A triode stacks a control grid between cathode and plate; small grid voltage swings large plate current. Add screen and suppressor grids to tame capacitance and secondary emission — tetrodes and pentodes stabilise RF performance. Cathode-ray tubes steer electron beams for oscilloscopes and television with electric and magnetic fields.

Manufacturing joins glass to kovar pins, winds molybdenum grids on jigs, and flashes barium getters that bind stray gas. Pump trains rough with rotary pumps, finish with diffusion or turbomolecular stacks, bake envelopes to drive water out of glass.

Power tubes dissipate kilowatts on graphite or metal anodes cooled by air, water, or vapor phases; bias supplies set operating points on plate curves chosen graphically before SPICE.

Logistics once measured tube counts like ammunition — ENIAC-scale computers were maintenance marathons as much as mathematical marvels.

principles.name: Child–Langmuir law
principles.explanation: Space-charge-limited current scales as three-halves power of plate voltage in planar diode idealisations.

principles.name: Transconductance
principles.explanation: Small-signal plate current change per grid voltage change sets gain with chosen plate load resistance.

principles.name: Miller effect
principles.explanation: Plate-to-grid capacitance multiplies effective input capacitance — motivates tetrode/pentode and later neutralisation in RF amps.

principles.name: Secondary emission
principles.explanation: High-energy electrons knock secondaries from grids — causes negative resistance regions exploited in dynodes but parasitic in tetrodes without suppressor grids.

components.name: Heated cathode
components.function: Thermionic emitter coated with oxides for low work function

components.name: Control and screen grids
components.madeFrom: Molybdenum wire wound on notched rods

components.name: Anode plate
components.madeFrom: Nickel or graphite with radiation fins in power tubes

components.name: Getter ring
components.function: Chemically pumps residual gas after seal-off

rawMaterials.name: Glass tubing and press
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Borosilicate glass tubing from lampworking supply; metal-ceramic packages for rugged military tubes

rawMaterials.name: Nickel and molybdenum
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Nickel sleeves and molybdenum grid wire from refined sulphide ores; hydrogen-fired cleanliness before assembly

rawMaterials.name: Pump oils and mercury historically
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Dry roughing pumps plus high-vacuum pumps; avoid mercury where environmental rules forbid it — use turbomolecular stacks when power allows

buildSteps.title: Machine electrode stacks with obsessive cleanliness
buildSteps.description: Wind grids with even pitch; align coaxially under magnification; spot-weld supports without splatter. Coat cathodes in controlled carbonate slurries; bake schedules matter.

buildSteps.title: Seal glass envelopes with leak-check discipline
buildSteps.description: Use graded glass-to-metal seals; anneal to relieve stress. Helium leak-check before pumping investment — a pinhole wastes days.
buildSteps.warningNote: Implosion shards are real — shield during tests

buildSteps.title: Pump, bake, and flash getters on schedule
buildSteps.description: Bake-out drives moisture from mica and glass; ion gauges tell truth about vacuum. Flash getter when pressure is right — too early wastes capacity.
buildSteps.warningNote: Mercury diffusion pumps need cold traps or they poison tubes

buildSteps.title: Age cathodes and plot curves
buildSteps.description: Run at prescribed over-temperature briefly to stabilise emission; trace plate curves on a graph, marking bias and dissipation limits in red.
buildSteps.warningNote: Red-plate glow means runaway — cut power

buildSteps.title: Bin tubes by gm and noise for intended service
buildSteps.description: Audio pairs need matched transconductance; RF stages need low microphonics. Print type numbers legibly; store upright to avoid cathode sag in large bottles.
buildSteps.warningNote: Dropping a power tube can bend grids internally — invisible until power applied

history.event: Fleming valve diode enables rectification for early radio.
De Forest Audion triode introduces grid control.
ENIAC-class machines deploy thousands of tubes for digital switching.

impact:
Tubes carried voice across continents, lit radar screens, and ran first computers. Their obsolescence in consumer gear does not erase their pedagogical clarity: visible electrodes, measurable potentials.

High-power RF transmitters still use descendants (klystrons, magnetrons) in niche roles.

videos.title: Vacuum tube operation
videos.description: Physics lectures on thermionic emission and triode curves.

sources.title: NIST SP 811
sources.note: Electrical units for vacuum-tube circuit analysis teaching

sources.title: Nature (1953) — Physical realization of amplification via DOI
sources.note: Historic peer-reviewed letter on semiconductor junction devices; contextualises tube-to-solid-state transition
